affordances.name: Shape conditions for local adaptation
affordances.mechanism: When outcomes emerge from many local interactions rather than a single central plan, use self-organization to design rules, incentives, information flows, guardrails, and feedback loops that let adaptive order emerge without abandoning governance.

use_when: Local interactions matter more than top-down instruction.
Leadership can shape conditions but cannot micromanage every move.
The goal is adaptive order under uncertainty.
do_not_use_when: Emergence is being used as an excuse for design neglect.
The team has not specified goals, feedback, or guardrails.
A linear blueprint would be more appropriate than decentralized adaptation.
case_evidence_needed: The interacting local agents or components.
The rule, incentive, information-flow, or feedback condition shaping interaction.
The goal or boundary condition that keeps adaptation useful.
The guardrail that prevents drift or chaos.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to name the local interactions and the governing conditions that shape them, instead of pretending emergence needs no design.
evidence_required: The rule, incentive, information flow, or feedback loop to adjust.
The goal, guardrail, or stop condition.
good_output_shape: Avoids top-down micromanagement and design neglect.

diagnostic_questions: Which local interactions are creating the observed order?
What rule, incentive, information flow, or feedback loop is shaping those interactions?
What guardrail keeps self-organization from becoming unmanaged drift?
misuse_guards: Do not invoke emergence to avoid specifying goals or guardrails.
Do not use a linear blueprint for a genuinely adaptive system.
Do not let decentralized order become an excuse for absent governance.

source_evidence.source_quote: Most useful when local interactions matter more than top-down instruction
the real leverage is in rules, incentives, information flows, and feedback loops
Danger When Emergence Is Used as an Excuse for Design Neglect

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 16 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports self-organization as condition shaping under uncertainty, not no-design ideology.
dropped_material: Did not extract emergence as a reason to avoid leadership or governance.
Did not turn multi-agent system analogies into runtime architecture changes.
open_questions: Later packet review should test whether this card stays distinct from complex-adaptive-systems and feedback-loops.

absence_records.reason: The source explicitly requires goals, feedback, and guardrails; it does not support unmanaged self-organization.
The source says leadership should shape conditions; it does not support treating emergent order as proof that design is unnecessary.

source_evidence.source_quote: leaders invoke self-organization to avoid specifying goals, feedback, or guardrails, leaving the system to drift rather than adapt intelligently.

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 16 source reading found strong reviewed depth for emergent order as local-interaction design with guardrails. PR79 hardens the no-design-needed absence with exact evidence about goals, feedback, guardrails, and drift.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, deterministic pressure, agent architecture, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: A later packet should compare this model against authority-bias when top-down control suppresses adaptation.
